deconstruction_targets: the notion that profit derives from selling commodities at prices above their value
the idea that profits are based on the price of commodities rather than labor
the assumption that there exist buyers and sellers with balanced roles in production
the concept of 'value of labour' as commonly understood
constructive_proposals: defining the value of labour by its relation to necessary labour time
starting from the theorem that commodities sell at their real values on average
explaining profits as derived from selling commodities in proportion to the quantity of labor realized in them
summary: The text deconstructs classical economic assumptions about value and profit by exposing their paradoxical foundations, arguing that profits arise from labor, not price differentials, while maintaining a highly charged moral tone that frames capitalist relations as inherently exploitative and self-contradictory.
